name: California Check Cashing Stores
description_short: California Check Cashing Stores at 707 Shaw Ave, Clovis, CA 93612 is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.9 stars across 570 reviews. Call +1 559-297-6700.
description_long:
California Check Cashing Stores operates multiple locations throughout San Francisco, including branches at 2531 Mission Street, 1098 Market Street, and 5129 Mission Street. The company positions itself as a one-stop financial services provider for underbanked consumers seeking quick access to cash and money services. Their primary focus is serving customers who need immediate liquidity through check cashing and small-dollar lending products.

The company offers a range of financial services including check cashing, payroll advances up to $250, bill payment services, tax preparation, Western Union money transfers and money orders, and Green Dot Visa debit cards. Payday loans are available both in-store and online. To apply for a loan, customers must provide a government-issued photo ID, Social Security number, proof of income, checking account information, and bank routing number, and be at least 18 years old.

California Check Cashing differentiates itself through multiple physical locations in high-traffic San Francisco neighborhoods, extended operating hours (9 AM–7 PM weekdays, 10 AM–4 PM Saturday), and the ability to apply for loans online in addition to in-store services. The company emphasizes convenience and accessibility for busy urban customers.

Important caveats exist: the website includes a consumer notice warning that small-dollar loans used over extended periods can be expensive, indicating potential high costs associated with their payday loan products. Loan amounts and approval depend on ability to repay and underwriting criteria, and not all products or services are available at every location. Terms, rates, and fees are referenced but not disclosed on the public website.
pros: Multiple San Francisco locations for convenient access (Mission Street, Market Street, and additional branches)
Extended hours including Saturday service (10 AM–4 PM) for working customers
Online loan application option available in addition to in-store applications
Payroll advances available up to $250 with same-day or quick funding
Comprehensive service suite including bill pay, money orders, tax prep, and Western Union transfers
Green Dot debit card option for customers needing banking access
Accessible requirements: only needs government ID, proof of income, and bank account information
cons: Payday loans carry high costs — company's own website warns that small-dollar loans used over time 'can be expensive'
Rates and fees not disclosed publicly on website; customers must visit store or contact directly
Loan approval not guaranteed; depends on 'ability to repay and other underwriting criteria' with no transparency on approval rates
Limited loan amount ($250 maximum for payroll advances) restricts utility for larger emergency needs
Services and products vary by location, creating uncertainty about availability at specific branches
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked San Francisco residents needing check cashing services without a bank account
Salaried employees seeking small payroll advances ($100–$250) before payday with quick funding
Customers needing multiple money services in one location (bill pay, wire transfers, tax prep, debit cards)
Workers in cash-heavy industries who need to convert checks to cash quickly and conveniently
diagnosis: California Check Cashing Stores is best for unbanked San Francisco residents needing check cashing and quick $100–$250 payroll advances with same-day access. The critical caveat is that payday loans carry high costs, as the company itself warns; customers should exhaust alternatives (employer advances, credit union PALs, credit cards) before using these products, as long-term reliance on high-cost short-term loans can create a debt cycle.
services: Green Dot Visa reloadable debit cards
Photo ID and proof of income verification
